title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
problem_statement: Search a target value in a rotated sorted array that may contain duplicates. Return true if the target exists.
intuition: Modified binary search handles duplicates by shrinking the search interval when the ordered half cannot be determined.

approaches.name: Binary Search with Duplicates
approaches.description: Compare mid to left and right to determine which half is sorted, and skip duplicates when ambiguous.

interview_qa.q: Why can this degrade to O(n)?
interview_qa.a: When duplicates hide the sorted half, the algorithm may need to shrink the interval linearly.

interview_qa.q: What if left, mid, and right are equal?
interview_qa.a: In that case, move both bounds inward to reduce ambiguity.
